idea-plugin.name: Git Branch Knife
idea-plugin.description:
Git Branch Knife — Split a Giant Git Branch into Focused PRs

Working in a monorepo? Made changes across multiple services in one branch? Git Branch Knife lets you visually split your Git branch changes into multiple focused, reviewable pull requests — one per service or module — directly inside IntelliJ IDEA.

No more giant PRs that touch 10 services at once. No more reviewers saying "this is too big to review."

The Problem It Solves

You're on feature/cross-service-update and your changes span payment-service, order-service, and auth-service. To get proper code reviews, you need three separate branches and PRs — but creating them manually is tedious and error-prone. Git Branch Knife does it in one click.

How It Works

  1. Trigger Slice by Service from the Git menu, Tools → Git Branch Knife, or right-click a branch in the Git Log.
  2. Your changed files appear in a tree on the left.
  3. Branch target cards are auto-created on the right — one per service, based on your branch-knife.paths config.
  4. Adjust assignments if needed, then click Execute Split.

Key Features

  • Visual branch splitter — See all changed files and assign them to target branches with a clear card-based UI.
  • Auto-detection — Automatically groups files by service using a simple path config file (branch-knife.paths).
  • Unassigned file tracking — Files not yet assigned to any branch are highlighted red in the left tree. Collapsed directories also turn red so nothing slips through.
  • Smart branch naming — New branches are named {current-branch}-{service}, e.g. feature/my-work-payment-service.
  • Hierarchical file tree — Files inside each card are shown as a collapsible tree, not a flat list.
  • Move all files — Bulk-move all files from one card to another in one click; the empty card is removed automatically.
  • Keyboard support — Press Delete / ⌫ to remove selected files or whole directory groups from a card.
  • Right-click context menu — Move single files or entire directories to another branch target.
  • Zero IDE restart — Edit branch-knife.paths and re-open the dialog; changes take effect immediately.
  • English / Chinese UI — Toggle language in the top-right corner of the dialog.

branch-knife.paths — Path Config Format

# path-prefix=branch-name, one rule per line
apps/payment-service=payment-service
apps/order-service=order-service
apps/auth-service=auth-service
# multiple paths → one branch:
apps/order-service/school=school-service

Files not matching any rule go into an Others card for manual assignment.

Entry Points

  • Git menu → Slice by Service…
  • Tools → Git Branch Knife → Slice by Service…
  • VCS Operations popup (Alt+`)
  • Right-click a branch in the Git Log branch panel
  • Find Action (⌘⇧A / Ctrl+Shift+A) → Slice by Service

No default shortcut — bind one in Settings → Keymap → Git Branch Knife.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.2

  • Fixed binary incompatibility with IDEA 2024.3 and 2025.1 (GitBranchActionsUtil API changes)
  • Replaced deprecated UIUtil.getSeparatorColor() and GitRepository.getGitDir()

0.1.1

  • Added plugin icon
  • Bug fixes and UI polish

0.1.0

  • Smart PR Splitter dialog with visual branch target cards
  • Hierarchical path tree view inside each card
  • Unassigned file tracking with red highlights in the left file tree
  • Move-all-files action with automatic source card removal
  • Delete key support for removing paths from cards
  • Directory-level deletion (removes all files under a dir)
  • Row hover highlight via alpha compositing
  • Smart branch naming based on current branch + service name
  • branch-knife.paths: unmatched files go to a single Others card (no further splitting)
  • Right-click context menus for move and remove

action.description: Split current branch changes into multiple focused branches by service path

group.description: Split monorepo branches by service path
